description: Strelitzia reginae, commonly known as the crane flower, bird of paradise, or isigude in Nguni, is a species of flowering plant native to the Cape Provinces and KwaZulu-Natal in South Africa. An evergreen perennial, it is widely cultivated for its dramatic flowers. In temperate areas it is a popular houseplant.

care.water_frequency: Every 7-10 days when top 2 inches are dry
care.light_preferred: Bright direct to indirect light (6+ hours)
care.soil_types: Well-draining potting mix with perlite and sand
care.repot_frequency: Every 2-3 years, large pot needed (grows big)
care.tips: Dramatic bird-like flowers require maturity (5+ years) and bright light. Grows large (1.5m+). Fan-shaped leaf arrangement. Slow grower.
care.toxicity_note: Mildly toxic if ingested by pets
common_problems: No flowers (too young, not enough light)
